# ghflow_commit (Resource)

Commits a single file to a branch in a GitHub repository.

This resource creates one commit per apply. Because git history is append-only, any change to its inputs forces a new commit (replacement), and `destroy` only removes the resource from state — it does not revert the commit.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `branch` (String) Branch to commit to. If it does not exist and `from_branch` is set, it is created first.
- `commit_message` (String) Commit message.
- `content` (String) UTF-8 file content to commit.
- `owner` (String) Owner of the repository (user or organization).
- `path` (String) Path of the file within the repository, e.g. `config/app.yaml`.
- `repository` (String) Name of the repository.

### Optional

- `author_email` (String) Commit author email. Defaults to the authenticated user if omitted.
- `author_name` (String) Commit author name. Defaults to the authenticated user if omitted.
- `from_branch` (String) If set and `branch` does not exist, create `branch` from this branch's HEAD before committing. Typical pattern: commit to a new feature branch created from `main`.

### Read-Only

- `commit_sha` (String) SHA of the created commit.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, set to the created commit SHA.
- `tree_sha` (String) SHA of the git tree created for the commit.
